messages.content:
rewrite: Dear Ms. Douglas, 

My name is <PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>, and I am writing on behalf of Neicie White. We have previously requested transcripts and student transfer records for Heaven White formerly (Booker T. Washington) and Bobby White formerly (Workman Middle School) on August 29, 2023.  Mrs. White was informed that Ms. Gwendolyn Thompson (Warrick) would be contacting Booker T. Washington High School and Workman Middle School for the necessary documentation. 
However, as of today’s date, we have not received any records from your school. Heaven White is now in her second week out of school, and we would greatly appreciate it if you could expedite this process. It is important for Heaven’s academic progression that we have these records as soon as possible.

If there are any questions or concerns regarding this request, please do not hesitate to reach out to me. I have copied both schools on this email strand to ensure clarity and facilitate communication.

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

Dear Ms. Douglas,

I am writing to follow up on our previous request for transcripts and student transfer records for Heaven White (formerly Booker T. Washington) and Bobby White (formerly Workman Middle School), on behalf of Neicie White. Our initial request was made on August 29, 2023, and we were informed that Ms. Gwendolyn Thompson (Warrick) would be contacting Booker T. Washington High School and Workman Middle School to obtain the necessary documentation.

Unfortunately, despite the time that has passed since our request, we have not yet received any records from your school. As Heaven White is now in her second week without school records, we kindly request that you expedite this process. Timely receipt of these records is crucial for Heaven's academic progress.

Should you have any questions or concerns regarding this matter, please feel free to reach out to me. I have included both schools in this email thread to ensure clarity and facilitate communication.

Thank you for your attention to this urgent matter.

Sincerely,
<PRESIDIO_ANONYMIZED_PERSON>

rewrite: Mackenzie has a medical diagnosis of ADHD and therefore receives special educational services under the category of Specific Learning Disability in order to access the general curriculum.  Specific learning Disability impacts Mackenzie's math and English general curriculum.  Her deficits in these areas of reading and listening comprehension, writing mechanics and math calculations are effected.
She requires testing accommodations such as small group testing, read aloud/audio, and simplifying/clarifying directions.  During instruction she receives audio/read aloud, extra time to process information, visual or graphic organizers, and alternate ways to present information assigned in methods such as video, one to one with instructor, or in a small group to help with anxiety

Mackenzie has been diagnosed with ADHD, which has led her to receive special educational services under the category of Specific Learning Disability. This qualifies her for assistance in accessing the general curriculum. Specifically, Mackenzie's math and English subjects are impacted by her Specific Learning Disability.

Mackenzie faces challenges in reading and listening comprehension, writing mechanics, and math calculations due to her disability. To accommodate her during testing, she requires certain adjustments. These include small group testing, the option of having directions read aloud or provided in audio format, as well as simplification and clarification of instructions.

During regular instruction, Mackenzie receives additional support. This includes having audio or read-aloud materials, extra time to process information, the use of visual or graphic organizers, and alternate methods of presenting information. These alternatives can include videos, one-on-one sessions with an instructor, or participation in small group activities. These accommodations aim to alleviate any anxiety she may experience as a result of her disability.

rewrite:  As of September 5, 2023, Mackenzie has only been in school 1 week.  According to her teachers, she is on task and shows no signs of adverse behaviors, however her previous progress report are as follows:

Mackenzie need to increase her reading prompt goal by identifying the source viewpoint and purpose of texts with 80% accuracy as measured by running records, classwork and assessments.
She also struggle in the area of writing when a prompt is given and Mackenzie is asked to show relationships between claims, reasons and evidence and include a conclusion that follows logical information 80% accuracy as measured by writing samples.

Mackenzie has shown sufficient improvement in decoding unfamiliar words.  She also increased improvement with multistep math problem by selecting and using mathematical strategies to solve problems.

As of September 5, 2023, Mackenzie has completed one week of school. According to her teachers, she has been focused and has not displayed any negative behaviors. However, her most recent progress reports indicate areas that require improvement:

In the area of reading, Mackenzie needs to work on increasing her proficiency in identifying the source viewpoint and purpose of texts. The goal is for her to achieve 80% accuracy in this skill, as measured by running records, classwork, and assessments.

Similarly, Mackenzie faces challenges in writing when presented with prompts that require her to demonstrate the relationships between claims, reasons, and evidence. Additionally, she needs to include a conclusion that follows logical information. The goal is for her to achieve 80% accuracy in this skill, assessed through writing samples.

On a positive note, Mackenzie has displayed significant improvement in decoding unfamiliar words. Furthermore, she has shown growth in solving multistep math problems, as she is now able to select and use appropriate mathematical strategies to solve them.

The progress Mackenzie has made thus far is encouraging, and with continued focus and support, we anticipate further improvement in these areas.
rewrite: Mackenzie is starting off to a great year, no evidence thus shows any lack of progress.
Mackenzie has begun the school year on a positive note, and there is no evidence to suggest any lack of progress thus far.
write a reading goal for a 12 grade student with specific learning disability

Reading Goal for 12th Grade Student with Specific Learning Disability:

By the end of the school year, our aim is for the student to improve their reading comprehension skills. This will be measured through their ability to analyze and interpret text, make inferences and draw conclusions, and demonstrate an understanding of main ideas and supporting details.

Specific objectives to achieve this goal include:
1. Increasing the student's ability to identify and analyze literary elements such as theme, symbolism, and characterization in both fiction and nonfiction texts.
2. Enhancing their skill in recognizing and using context clues to determine the meaning of unfamiliar words or phrases.
3. Developing their ability to effectively summarize and synthesize information from texts of varying complexity and content.
4. Strengthening their critical thinking skills by evaluating and comparing multiple texts on a similar topic or theme.
5. Expanding their vocabulary by learning and incorporating new words encountered in their reading.

To assess progress, regular reading comprehension assessments and discussions will be conducted throughout the year. Students will also have opportunities to engage in independent reading and participate in guided reading activities to practice these skills.

With targeted instruction, support, and consistent practice, we are confident that the student will make significant improvements in their reading comprehension abilities by the end of the 12th grade year.
Math Goal for 12th Grade Student with Specific Learning Disability:

Math Goal for 12th Grade Student with Specific Learning Disability:

By the end of the school year, our objective is for the student to improve their proficiency in mathematical concepts and problem-solving skills, with a focus on the areas impacted by their specific learning disability.

Specific objectives to achieve this goal include:
1. Increasing the student's ability to apply mathematical concepts and formulas in real-world situations, such as finance, measurement, or data analysis.
2. Enhancing their understanding and proficiency in algebraic operations, including solving equations and inequalities, manipulating expressions, and graphing functions.
3. Developing their skills in problem-solving strategies, including identifying relevant information, choosing appropriate problem-solving methods, and evaluating the reasonableness of solutions.
4. Strengthening their ability to interpret and analyze graphs, charts, and tables, and effectively communicate mathematical reasoning.
5. Improving their mathematical fluency, including accuracy and efficiency in computations, as well as mental math skills.

To assess progress, regular math assessments, both written and verbal, will be conducted throughout the year. The student will also have opportunities to apply their math skills in real-world scenarios and engage in practical problem-solving activities.

With targeted instruction, accommodations, and ongoing support, we are confident that the student will make significant improvements in their mathematical abilities by the end of the 12th grade year.
write: Goal for 12th Grade Student with Specific Learning Disability

Goal for 12th Grade Student with Specific Learning Disability:

Our goal for the 12th grade student with a specific learning disability is to support their overall academic success and ensure they have the necessary skills to transition into post-secondary education, work, or other appropriate opportunities. This will involve addressing their specific learning needs while fostering independence and self-advocacy.

Specific objectives for achieving this goal may include:

1. Developing effective study skills and organizational strategies to manage assignments, deadlines, and materials independently.
2. Improving time management and task prioritization to ensure successful completion of academic responsibilities.
3. Enhancing their written expression skills, such as grammar, punctuation, and sentence structure, to enable effective communication in various academic and professional settings.
4. Strengthening their oral communication skills, including public speaking and effective presentation techniques.
5. Providing necessary accommodations and modifications to support their learning, such as extended time for assignments and assessments, preferential seating, or the use of assistive technology.
6. Fostering self-advocacy skills, including the ability to communicate needs, seek assistance when necessary, and effectively utilize available resources and support systems.
7. Promoting a growth mindset and resilience, helping the student develop a positive attitude towards challenges and setbacks, and encouraging perseverance.
8. Facilitating career exploration and post-secondary planning, assisting the student in identifying their strengths, interests, and potential career pathways.

Regular assessments, progress monitoring, and individualized support will be utilized to track the student's progress towards these objectives. Collaborative communication between the student, parents, teachers, and support staff will ensure ongoing adjustments and tailored interventions as needed.

With a holistic approach to address their unique learning profile and maximizing their potential, we are committed to helping this student achieve their academic and future personal and professional goals.
write :Goal for 12th Grade Student with Specific Learning Disability:

Goal for 12th Grade Student with Specific Learning Disability:

Our goal for the 12th grade student with a specific learning disability is to enhance their academic skills and ensure they have the necessary tools to succeed in their final year of high school, as well as in their future endeavors. 

Specific objectives for achieving this goal may include:

1. Improving reading comprehension skills by analyzing and interpreting complex texts, identifying main ideas and supporting details, and making inferences.
2. Developing effective writing skills by demonstrating proper grammar, sentence structure, and organization in various types of writing tasks, such as essays, research papers, and reports.
3. Enhancing critical thinking and problem-solving abilities, particularly in the areas impacted by their specific learning disability, such as math or logical reasoning.
4. Strengthening self-advocacy skills to empower the student to communicate their needs and seek appropriate accommodations or support when necessary.
5. Fostering independence and self-reliance to ensure the student can complete assignments, manage their time effectively, and meet deadlines.
6. Encouraging collaborative learning and participation in group projects, discussions, and presentations to develop communication and teamwork skills.
7. Providing targeted remediation or additional support in areas where the student requires extra assistance or reinforcement.
8. Assisting the student in setting realistic short-term and long-term goals for their academic and personal growth.

Regular progress monitoring, ongoing communication, and individualized support will be implemented to track the student's progress towards these objectives. Collaborative involvement from teachers, parents, and support staff will ensure a comprehensive approach to meet the student's needs.

With targeted interventions, accommodations, and a supportive environment, our aim is to empower the student with the skills and confidence needed to thrive academically and successfully transition to their chosen next steps beyond high school.
write a writing goal for Mackenzie who is a 12th grade student with specific learning disability

Writing Goal for Mackenzie, a 12th Grade Student with Specific Learning Disability:

The goal for Mackenzie is to improve her writing skills, specifically focusing on the areas impacted by her specific learning disability. Our aim is for her to effectively express her thoughts and ideas in writing, while also demonstrating proper grammar, sentence structure, and organization.

Specific objectives for achieving this goal include:

1. Enhancing the ability to generate and organize ideas before writing, through strategies such as brainstorming, outlining, or graphic organizers.
2. Improving sentence structure to create clear and grammatically correct sentences, including the appropriate use of punctuation and capitalization.
3. Developing paragraph development skills, including the use of topic sentences, supporting details, and concluding sentences.
4. Strengthening skills in providing evidence and coherent reasoning to support her arguments or claims in persuasive or analytical writing pieces.
5. Increasing the ability to revise and edit her writing for clarity, coherence, and coherence, including identifying and correcting grammar, spelling, or punctuation errors.
6. Expanding vocabulary and incorporating appropriate language conventions to enhance the sophistication and clarity of her writing.
7. Building self-confidence as a writer, encouraging Mackenzie to take risks, seek feedback, and view writing as a means of self-expression.

Regular formative assessments, writing samples, and individualized feedback will be used to monitor Mackenzie's progress towards these objectives. Collaborative communication between Mackenzie, her teachers, and support staff will ensure ongoing adjustments and targeted interventions as needed.

With targeted instruction, accommodations, and consistent support, we are confident that Mackenzie will make significant improvements in her writing skills, enabling her to effectively communicate her ideas in writing and succeed academically and beyond.
rewrite: Mackenzie will not participate in reading and math resource with her peers due to her deficits in math and English curriculum
Given Mackenzie's deficits in the math and English curriculum, it has been determined that she will not participate in reading and math resource activities with her peers.

To accommodate her during testing, she requires certain adjustments. These include small group testing, the option of having directions read aloud or provided in audio format, as well as simplification and clarification of instructions.

During regular instruction, Mackenzie receives additional support. Given Mackenzie's difficulties in reading and listening comprehension, writing mechanics, and math calculations due to her specific learning disability, certain accommodations are necessary to support her during testing and regular instruction.

During testing, it is important to provide Mackenzie with appropriate accommodations. These include conducting tests in small group settings, reading directions aloud or providing them in audio format, and simplifying and clarifying instructions to ensure comprehension.

In regular instruction, Mackenzie requires additional support to help her overcome these challenges. This involves providing her with audio or read-aloud materials, allowing extra time for information processing, utilizing visual or graphic organizers, and employing alternative methods of presenting information. These alternatives can include using videos as teaching aids, providing one-on-one sessions with instructors, or encouraging participation in small group activities. By implementing these accommodations, we aim to reduce any anxiety that Mackenzie may experience as a result of her disability.
